title: ctenolophon parvifolius
intro: Ctenolophon parvifolius, a plant species commonly known as the "African boxwood," is a flowering plant in the family Ctenolophonaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in various parts of Africa for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Ctenolophon parvifolius include its potential to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its use in wound healing and skin conditions. The plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion by boiling its leaves and roots in water, which can be used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
